context: Tests whether the solver applies Swift API naming for side effects, mutating/nonmutating pairs, direct-object present participles, noun/form-noun operations, and public documentation comments.

checklist.name: Sort pair
checklist.description: Uses an imperative mutating sort name and a past-participle nonmutating sort name, such as `sortLines()` and `sortedLines()`.

checklist.name: No form sort
checklist.description: Does not keep or recommend `formSortLines()` for the verb-described sort operation.

checklist.name: Newline pair
checklist.description: Uses `strippingNewlines()` or an equivalent present-participle name for the copy-returning newline-removal operation.

checklist.name: Direct-object rationale
checklist.description: Explains that the present participle is appropriate for the newline-removal operation because the verb takes a direct object or because the `-ed` form is ungrammatical in that pattern.

checklist.name: Union pair
checklist.description: Uses noun/form-noun pairing for the tag union operation: nonmutating `union...` and mutating `formUnion...`.

checklist.name: No returning form
checklist.description: Does not present a `formUnion...` method that returns the combined set as the corrected nonmutating design.

checklist.name: Doc comments
checklist.description: Adds documentation comments for every public member shown in the revised declaration.

checklist.name: Summary kinds
checklist.description: Documentation summaries describe methods by what they do or return and describe the computed property by what it is.

checklist.name: Complexity note
checklist.description: Documents nonconstant complexity for `totalCharacterCount` or clearly states why a complexity note is needed for that computed property.

checklist.name: No implementation drift
checklist.description: Keeps the answer focused on public API design rather than adding unrelated package architecture or UI code.
